Angular continues to evolve toward a more intuitive and developer-friendly API surface. Following the success of decorators like @Component, @Directive, and @Pipe, the Angular team is preparing to introduce a brand-new @Service decorator in Angular v22.
This upcoming feature aims to simplify service creation while making Angular’s architecture more consistent and easier to understand.
Why Is Angular Introducing @Service?
For years, Angular developers have used @Injectable() to create services:
import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';@Injectable({
providedIn: 'root'
})
export class InventoryApi {
fetchItems() {
return [];
}
}
While this works perfectly, the name Injectable often creates confusion, especially for developers new to Angular.
Technically, many Angular constructs are injectable:

• Components

• Directives

• Pipes

• Services
Yet only services commonly use the @Injectable() decorator.
The new @Service() decorator provides a clearer semantic meaning:
import { Service } from '@angular/core';@Service()…
